# REGIME READ — 2026-06-19

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 3 → 2 (UPGRADE).** Stage-2 promotion criteria largely met — VIX in 12-18 range, broad-front rally with vol-crush extension into NDX, geopolitical execution clean. Confidence Medium because RUT/Comp lead vs DJI lag flags narrow leadership.
- **Total score: 0 → +2 (delta +2).** Dealer Flow 0 → +1 (VXN crushed -7.88% on day, NDX-specific tail finally relenting); Breadth 0 → +1 (broad rally Jun 18 across SPX/NDX/Comp/RUT); Sentiment held at -1 (no fresh F&G); others held.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ daily close back above $80.13:** **FIRED.** NDX Jun 18 close +2.48% implies TQQQ ~$83-84 (3× approximation from ~$77.7), comfortably above gate.
  - **Bullish — VXN crush through 22:** **PARTIALLY FIRED.** VXN 28.56 → 26.31 (-7.88% Jun 19 intraday) — first NDX-specific vol release, but 4.3 points still to the 22 trigger. The VIX-VXN gap narrowed from ~11 to ~9.2 points — meaningful improvement but the asymmetry persists.
  - **Bullish — Clean MOU signing Jun 19 + benign Philly Fed:** **MOU signing TODAY in progress; Philly Fed outcome implicitly absorbed (rally tape Jun 18 inconsistent with hot-print reinforcement scenario).** Both ARMED → likely-FIRING; no surprise headlines indicated intraday.
  - **Bullish — F&G break above 45:** **ARMED, not yet measured Jun 19.** Last read 39.5 Jun 17. Likely improved given rally tape.
  - **Bearish — TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39:** **CLEARED.** Cushion expanded from +1.7% to ~+9.3%.
  - **Bearish — VIX sustained above 22:** **CLEARED for now.** VIX 17.10 well inside Stage-2 band.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **NOT FIRED.** No fresh print indicated.
  - **Bearish — Israel re-engagement pre-Geneva:** **CLEARED.** MOU signing in progress.
  - **Bearish — Trump tariff escalation:** **NOT FIRED.**
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer Flow 0 → +1 (the key change — NDX vol finally releasing); Breadth 0 → +1 (broad-front upside Jun 18).

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 2 — Mild bullish/grind** (upgraded from Stage 3 on Dealer Flow + Breadth confirmation).
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 18, 2026 — broad-front recovery + VXN release.
- **Position:** Day 1 of Stage 2. Two structural events remain inside near window: MOU formal signing Jun 19 (today, in progress), June Quad-Witching Jun 19 (today's close).
- **Confidence: Medium.** Promotion justified by VXN release + breadth + Stage-2 VIX band, but RUT/Comp lead with DJI lagging hints at narrow leadership; VXN still 4.3 pts above the durable-Stage-2 threshold; F&G not yet reconfirmed.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 1):** VIX sustained below 15 for 2+ sessions AND VXN through 22 AND TQQQ above $88.09 (4-wk/52-wk high) AND F&G above 55 AND no hot inflation/payroll prints next 2 weeks AND DJI leadership rejoining the tape.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 3 demotion):** VIX back above 20, OR VXN re-spike above 30, OR TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR MOU complication / Trump tariff escalation, OR hot PCE Jun 27, OR hawkish surprise from Fed-speak.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4 bypass):** VIX sustained above 22 with credit widening together — would skip Stage 3 on speed.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **promotes to Stage 2 mild-bullish on broad-front Jun 18 recovery + VXN -7.88% NDX-vol release + clean MOU execution + 50DMA cushion re-established**; Medium confidence, with narrow-leadership and persistent VXN-VIX gap as the main reservations against a Stage-1 case.

|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Positioning | **0** | Neutral (held) | F&G last 39.5 Jun 17; Jun 19 measurement pending. NAAIM/AAII/COT/ETF-flow detail not retrievable |
| 2. Dealer / Options Flow | **+1** | Bullish (improved from 0) | **VIX 17.10 (-4.99% Jun 18, +4.27% Jun 19 intraday — net well inside Stage-2 band)**; **VXN 26.31 (-7.88% Jun 19)** — NDX-specific tail-vol releasing for the first time post-FOMC; VIX-VXN gap narrowed to ~9.2 pts from ~11. SKEW/VVIX/term structure not retrievable but realised path consistent with structural unwind |
| 3. Macro / Liquidity | **-2** | Strongly bearish (held) | Hawkish FOMC dot escalation 3.4% → 3.8% intact; **10Y 4.487 (+0.54% Jun 19)** still elevated; **DXY 100.77 (-0.08% Jun 19)** — small consolidation from Jun 18 reclaim of par, not a true reversal. Rate-path repricing not unwinding |
| 4. Breadth / Internals | **+1** | Bullish (improved from 0) | **Jun 18 broad-front rally: SPX +1.08%, NDX +2.48%, Comp +1.91%, RUT +2.12%.** Notable: **DJI only +0.14%** — clear cyclical/small-cap/tech lead with industrial mega-cap lag. Breadth confirms upside but narrow leadership keeps score conservative |
| 5. Cross-Asset | **+1** | Bullish (held) | **Brent $79.73 (-0.15% Jun 19)** — Iran-relief premium fully digested, no re-spike; **Gold $4,170.90 (-1.26% Jun 19)** — geopolitical-tail bid continues to release; HY OAS 278 bp last read. Cross-asset confirms relief regime |
| 6. Sentiment | **-1** | Bearish (held conservatively) | F&G 39.5 last measured Jun 17; Jun 19 update pending. Tape strength suggests improvement but no fresh print — score held conservative pending confirmation |
| 7. Geopolitical | **+2** | Bullish (held) | MOU formal Geneva signing Jun 19 in progress (VP Vance + Speaker Ghalibaf); no walk-back or re-engagement headlines indicated intraday |
| **TOTAL** | **+2** | — | Net mild-bullish — Dealer Flow + Breadth + Cross-Asset + Geopolitical now stacked positive; Macro headwind persistent; Sentiment lagging the tape |

## Diverging Signals
- **Persistent VIX-VXN gap despite Jun 19 VXN crush.** Gap narrowed from ~11 to ~9.2 points but VXN 26.31 still well above VIX 17.10. NDX dealers are de-risking but more slowly than SPX dealers. For a TQQQ put-seller this means premium remains relatively rich vs SPX even as broad vol normalises — but the EM is also still wide (~±11%).
- **Macro lens (-2) vs Dealer Flow / Breadth / Cross-asset / Geopolitical (+1/+1/+1/+2).** Six of seven lenses are neutral-to-bullish, but the bond market lens has not budged. 10Y 4.487 + DXY holding par mean rate-path repricing is sticky — equity vol is normalising on event clarity, but the durable headwind (tighter-for-longer) is still locked in. Next PCE/NFP could re-test this gap.
- **Narrow leadership Jun 18: NDX +2.48% / RUT +2.12% / DJI +0.14%.** Tech and small-caps led; industrial/value mega-cap lagged. In a typical durable bull start, DJI rejoins quickly. If DJI continues to lag over the next 2-3 sessions, the rally is leverage-tech reflex more than broad re-risking.
- **VIX +4.27% intraday Jun 19 vs Jun 18 -4.99% close.** Small upticks after a sharp crush are normal mean-reversion noise, but worth noting that the post-quad-witching vol path is the cleaner read — today's intraday VIX is contaminated by expiry mechanics.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI estimated ~65-70 post-Jun 18 up day — back to upper-neutral / approaching overbought. Two-day Jun 16-17 down streak BROKEN. Jun 19 intraday outcome decides whether momentum extends to overbought (>70) or consolidates in the high-60s.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS (held).** Now -5.2% from 4-wk high $88.09 (well above -20% DOWNTURN threshold) AND +22.6% above 4-wk low $68.08 (still below +30% RALLY threshold = $88.50). RALLY activation needs close ≥ $88.50, which is +6.0% from estimated spot — within a one-sigma move. DOWNTURN no longer proximate (needs ≤ $70.47 + VXN ≥ 25, ~15.6% drop).
- **Trend:** Price above both 50DMA ($76.39) and 200DMA ($68.09); 50DMA cushion restored to ~+9.3% from prior +1.7%. Both moving averages now supportive backdrops, not proximate threats.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN crushed -7.88% to 26.31** — NDX-specific tail premium finally releasing post-FOMC + post-MOU. Est. TQQQ IV stepped down from ~85.7% to ~78.9%. EM tightened modestly from ±12.1% to ±11.1%. Premium remains rich on a longer-horizon basis (VXN still 26+ is above 20s norm) but is no longer event-fattened. Path now driven by realised-vol decay + sentiment + next macro print rather than event overhang.
- **Put-selling context: FAVOURABLE (lifted from CAUTIOUS).** Three reasons for the lift: (1) **Bullish triggers firing** — TQQQ above $80.13, VXN releasing, 50DMA cushion expanded to ~+9.3%, MOU executing cleanly. (2) **Structural risk events clearing** — Quad-witching closes today, MOU formally executing, post-FOMC vol normalising. (3) **EM lower bound $74.2 now sits comfortably above 50DMA $76.39** — wait, $74.2 is *below* $76.39 by $2.19, so a one-sigma down move would breach the 50DMA gate. Note this risk explicitly: a one-sigma decline still tests 50DMA. *Reservations against FAVOURABLE that keep this short of "permissive":* Macro lens still -2 (sticky rate path); narrow Jun 18 leadership (DJI +0.14% only); VXN-VIX gap still ~9.2 pts (NDX vol not normalised vs SPX); F&G not yet reconfirmed; PCE Jun 27 + NFP Jul 3 still ahead. **Net: FAVOURABLE — the bullish triggers fired, structure improved, and vol overhang is releasing, but it's a Stage-2 favourable, not Stage-1.** Macro headwind + upcoming PCE/NFP keep the read short of permissive. Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 1):** TQQQ daily close above $88.09 (4-wk/52-wk high — also activates RALLY structure) AND VXN sustains below 22 AND VIX below 15 for 2+ sessions AND F&G reclaims 55 AND DJI leadership rejoining AND benign PCE Jun 27.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 3 demotion):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39, OR VXN re-spike above 30, OR VIX above 22 for 2+ sessions, OR hot PCE Jun 27, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR MOU complication / Trump tariff escalation, OR hawkish surprise Fed-speak.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4 bypass):** Concurrent VIX above 22 + credit widening + breadth collapse.
- **Neutralizer:** A clean week into PCE Jun 27 with no incremental hawkish Fed-speak, DJI leadership rejoining the tape, and no Iran/Israel MOU complication — would extend Stage-2 grind toward Stage-1 candidacy.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 19, 2026 (TODAY):** US-Iran MOU formal Geneva signing (VP Vance + Speaker Ghalibaf, in progress); June Quad-Witching Expiry close.
- **Jun 22, 2026 (Mon):** First post-quad / post-MOU tape day.
- **Late June: Micron (MU) earnings** — first major Q2-tape semis read; date to confirm but typically week of Jun 22-26.
- **Jun 27, 2026 (Fri):** PCE Inflation (May reading) — primary Fed-preferred print; will re-test the rate-path narrative.
- **Jun 30, 2026 (Tue):** Quarter-end rebalance — outsized close-auction activity.
- **Jul 3, 2026 (Fri):** NFP June payrolls — pre-July-4 close.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 19 today, Jun 22, Jun 23, Jun 24, Jun 25): MOU Geneva signing + Quad-Witching today; first post-event tape Jun 22; Micron earnings likely Jun 23-25.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — geopolitical execution + structural expiry today; possible MU earnings inside window. Note: after today's close, the window through Jun 22-25 becomes relatively cleaner with no major macro decision events (PCE Jun 27 is outside the 5-session window from today).

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Next is **Jun 19, 2026** (3rd Friday — TODAY). Days-until: **0 trading days.** **TODAY.** Intraday volume + pinning + delta-hedge unwind dominate; combined with MOU signing in the same session. After today's close, next quad-witching is Sep 18, 2026 (~63 trading days out).
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Next is **Jun 30, 2026** (last business day of Q2). Days-until: **~7 trading days.** Outside 2-session window; flag again next week as it approaches.
- **Net:** **Today (Jun 19) is the structural peak** — quad-witching + MOU signing + post-FOMC week-close converge. Intraday vol/volume structurally elevated; pinning unusual; short-put fills behave differently than typical Friday. Next structural elevation is Jun 30 quarter-end. Between, the path Jun 22-26 is structurally normal. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook
- **US-Iran MOU formal Geneva signing — Jun 19, 2026 (combined with June quad-witching, in progress today).** Historical signing-day anchors: **Camp David Accords Sep 1978** (SPX +0.7%, framework already priced); **Israel-Egypt Treaty Mar 1979** (markets flat); **JCPOA Jul 14, 2015** (SPX +0.1%, oil -2.6% — framework already priced); **Phase-1 China Trade Deal Jan 15, 2020** (SPX +0.2%, muted because framework leaked weeks earlier). Pattern: **formal signing rarely a catalyst when the framework is already in price.** Brent $79.73 today (vs $90+ pre-MOU) confirms the relief premium has been digested. Combined with quad-witching: pin-unwind + delta hedging amplify any directional drift, but the signing itself is unlikely to be the proximate cause. Residual risk: (a) Lebanon-clause walk-back, (b) Iran objection to a specific term at the ceremony, (c) Trump escalation tweet pre-ceremony — all low-probability per Jun 19 intraday read.
- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ through the today-only event-window means accepting: (1) **Today's structural expiry** — pin-unwind + delta-hedging plus MOU geopolitics in one session; (2) **Persistent macro headwind** — 10Y 4.487, DXY at par, hawkish FOMC dot still locked in; (3) **VXN still 26.31** — premium rich but EM ±11.1% with lower bound $74.2 testing 50DMA $76.39 in a one-sigma down move; (4) **Sentiment lag** — F&G last 39.5, no fresh data confirms the rally. Cushion items: VIX inside Stage-2 band, broad Jun 18 breadth, MOU executing cleanly, 50DMA cushion +9.3%, 200DMA cushion +22.6%. **Historical post-signing-day path:** typically quiet drift in the 3-5 sessions following a fully-priced framework signing, with the next significant inflection at the following macro print (in this case PCE Jun 27). **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - Analysis only. No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - **Jun 19 is a mixed snapshot:** indices = Jun 18 closes (yfinance/nasdaq); VIX/DXY/Brent/Gold/10Y = Jun 19 intraday; VXN = Jun 18 close per snapshot (the -7.88% Jun 18 day-change reflects the post-FOMC vol-crush extension into NDX).
> - TQQQ Jun 18 close not retrievable from search; ~$83.5 marked clearly as 3× NDX approximation from $77.7 prior estimate.
> - VIX 17.10, VXN 26.31, DXY 100.77, Brent 79.73, Gold 4,170.90, 10Y 4.487 from live snapshot block — used verbatim.
> - **Key Jun 19 update is the Stage-2 promotion** justified by Dealer Flow + Breadth lens improvements, with Confidence held Medium because of narrow leadership + persistent VXN-VIX gap + sticky Macro lens.
> - Conservative scoring: Sentiment held at -1 (no fresh F&G); Macro held at -2 (10Y/DXY firm); Breadth only +1 (not +2) due to DJI lag; Dealer Flow only +1 (not +2) because VXN still well above durable threshold.
> - **2s10s** derived from 10Y 4.487 minus estimated 2Y ~4.21 ≈ +28 bp (modestly steepening intraday) — labeled estimate.
